evals.prompt: We keep losing repo background between agent sessions. I need a persistent memory layer so the next coding agent can load the right docs, avoid repeated explanations, and store the conclusion after the task.
evals.expected_output: Selects OpenContext as the project/repo memory layer, explains a load → search → store workflow, and mentions manifests or searches plus post-run capture.
assertions: Output selects OpenContext or project/repo memory as the primary fit
Output includes a load/search/store or equivalent lifecycle
Output mentions a manifest, folder scan, or targeted document search
Output includes storing conclusions or handoff notes after meaningful work

evals.prompt: I want one place for persistent AI memory, but I'm not sure whether I should use OpenContext, a markdown wiki, Obsidian, or a graph tool for this project and future research.
evals.expected_output: Compares memory layers, picks OpenContext for active project memory when appropriate, and routes wiki, graph, and human-authored vault needs to the right adjacent tools.
assertions: Output distinguishes OpenContext from llm-wiki or a markdown wiki
Output distinguishes OpenContext from graph-style structure mapping
Output mentions a human-authored note/vault surface such as Obsidian when relevant
Output avoids claiming that OpenContext replaces every adjacent memory layer

evals.prompt: Set up OpenContext in our repo and tell me when I should bother with embeddings or semantic search instead of simple keyword search.
evals.expected_output: Provides installation/init guidance, core commands, and a bounded rule for escalating from keyword search to embeddings/index builds.
assertions: Output includes install and/or `oc init` guidance
Output includes at least one core OpenContext command such as `oc search` or `oc context manifest`
Output states that keyword search should be tried before escalating to embeddings or index builds
Output treats semantic search setup as conditional rather than mandatory
